filter_index.video_title: Complete Guide To Do The Everest Base Camp Trek On Your Own | Classic Route | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow patches may be present on the trail in Spring (April), generally above 12,000 feet
No snow on the trek in Autumn (October)
Spring (April): rain or sun behind clouds can drop daytime temperature to around 12 °C
Autumn (October): rain or slight drizzle brings down daytime temperature by a few degrees
temperature_notes: Spring (April) daytime: 18 °C to 24 °C; nighttime: 5 °C to 0 °C; 4 warm layers required
Autumn (October) daytime: 12 °C to 15 °C; nighttime: -5 °C to -10 °C; 5 warm layers required

terrain_challenges: Long distances over 100 km across 12 days
Boulder sections on steep, uneven trails
Boulder fields and glacial moraines (Dingboche to Lobuche)
Glacial moraine walks (Gorakshep to Everest Base Camp)
Uneven terrain punishing on knees (Tengboche descent)
altitude_or_weather_risks: Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) risk from Day 2 (Phakding to Namche Bazaar, ~3000 ft gain)
Very high altitudes up to 18,200 ft at Kala Patthar
AMS risk remains even with acclimatization stops at Namche Bazaar and Dingboche
AMS risk above 10,000 ft applies to anyone including first-time and experienced trekkers
Risk of HAPE and HACE referenced

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog, or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Trekking primarily uses lungs, core, calves, glutes, quads, and hamstrings
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
Only running, jogging, or walking are accepted as fitness proofs; cross-training workouts are not considered valid
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the trek's fitness criteria
Fitness records may be rejected if you fail to link your fitness profile or do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
A 6-week plan is provided for those not currently active

experience_themes: Spring (April) is moist with slightly warmer daytime temperatures and possible snow patches above 12,000 feet
Autumn (October) is dry and crisp with no snow on the trek and colder nights
Snow patches may be present on the trail in Spring (April), generally above 12,000 feet
No snow on the trek in Autumn (October)
primary_watchouts: Spring (April): rain or cloud cover can drop daytime temperature to around 12 °C
Autumn (October): nights are chilly, dipping to -5 °C; rain/drizzle lowers daytime temperature; keep a jacket handy
Long distances over 100 km across 12 days
Boulder sections on steep, uneven trails
Boulder fields and glacial moraines (Dingboche to Lobuche)
Glacial moraine walks (Gorakshep to Everest Base Camp)
not_ideal_for: trekkers with pre-existing medical conditions (must be disclosed)

video_title: Complete Guide To Do The Everest Base Camp Trek On Your Own | Classic Route | Indiahikes
